assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 28: 'Ecosystem: RPM'. The rationale explicitly identifies openssl-libs as a system RPM package: 'The vulnerable library openssl-libs is a system RPM package (not a Cargo crate, npm package, or Go module).' Supporting evidence includes RPM naming conventions, .el9_4 dist tags, RHSA advisory type, and reference to Ecosystem Mappings table listing RPM with rpms.lock.yaml as the lock file.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table for 'Stream: 2.2.x (rhtpa-release.0.4.z)' listing all five versions: 2.2.0 (3.0.7-25.el9_3, YES), 2.2.1 (3.0.7-27.el9_4, YES), 2.2.2 (retag of 2.2.1, YES), 2.2.3 (3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO), 2.2.4 (3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO). The dependency chain context section states 'rpms.lock.yaml confirms: openssl-libs present in lock file' and 'The package openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml for all checked versions'.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context classifies the package origin as explicit install because openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml, not inherited from the base image (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 22-31: 'Dependency Chain Context (RPM, explicit install)' section states 'rpms.lock.yaml confirms: openssl-libs present in lock file' and 'Origin: explicit install (package is listed in rpms.lock.yaml)'. Line 33 reiterates: 'The package openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml for all checked versions, making it an explicit install (not inherited from base image).'

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 5-6: 'Since openssl-libs is an RPM system package (not a source dependency), a single remediation task is created for the Konflux release repo. No upstream backport task is needed.' Only one jira.create_issue call is present (line 16-22), creating a single task with summary 'Remediate CVE-2026-40215: update openssl-libs to 3.0.7-28.el9_4 (rhtpa-2.2)'. The task description targets the Konflux release repo 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z'. No second task for upstream backport is created.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md task description contains all required sections: '## Repository' (rhtpa-release.0.4.z, line 29-30), '## Target Branch' (main, line 32-34), '## Description' (lines 36-46), '## Implementation Notes' (lines 48-55), '## Acceptance Criteria' (lines 57-59). No '## Files to Modify' section is present in the task description, consistent with remediation-templates.md which states 'Files to Modify and Files to Create are intentionally omitted. These depend on repository structure that the triage skill does not have context for.' The format matches the 'Explicit install origin' template from remediation-templates.md.
